title: Lepisorus abbreviatus
taxonomy: Lepisorus abbreviatus is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It belongs to the class Polypodiopsida, a group of vascular plants commonly known as polypodiophytes. The class Polypodiopsida is further divided into several orders, with Lepisorus abbreviatus being a member of the order Polypodiales. This species is native to certain regions and is classified based on its morphological characteristics and genetic makeup. Further research may be needed to confirm the exact classification of Lepisorus abbreviatus.

intro: Lepisorus abbreviatus, also known as the Short-stalked Lipfern or Short-stalked Chinese Lipfern, is a species of fern in the genus Lepisorus, native to China. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it useful in the treatment of various conditions such as arthritis and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Lepisorus abbreviatus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Lepisorus abbreviatus is often used in herbal preparations such as infusions and decoctions, as well as in the form of capsules and tablets, to promote overall health and well-being.
